# CWE-1204 safe: random IV per encryption
lang: go
file: CWE-1204-safe.go
---
package sample

// CWE-1204 mitigated: crypto/rand fills a unique IV for each ciphertext.

import (
	"crypto/aes"
	"crypto/cipher"
	"crypto/rand"
	"encoding/base64"
	"io"
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

func EncryptFieldSafe(key []byte) gin.HandlerFunc {
	return func(c *gin.Context) {
		plain := c.PostForm("value")
		block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
		if err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
			return
		}
		iv := make([]byte, block.BlockSize())
		if _, err := io.ReadFull(rand.Reader, iv); err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
			return
		}
		mode := cipher.NewCBCEncrypter(block, iv)
		padded := pkcs7PadSafe([]byte(plain), block.BlockSize())
		out := make([]byte, len(padded))
		mode.CryptBlocks(out, padded)
		payload := append(iv, out...)
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"ciphertext": base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(payload)})
	}
}

func pkcs7PadSafe(data []byte, blockSize int) []byte {
	pad := blockSize - len(data)%blockSize
	padding := make([]byte, pad)
	for i := range padding {
		padding[i] = byte(pad)
	}
	return append(data, padding...)
}
